hints: An aggregate function takes a whole column and boils it down to one value -- SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX all work this way, the same as COUNT.
Add SUM(salary), AVG(salary), MIN(salary), and MAX(salary) as more columns in the SELECT list, each with its own AS alias.
SELECT COUNT(*) AS headcount, SUM(salary) AS total_payroll, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary, MIN(salary) AS lowest, MAX(salary) AS highest FROM employee;
